cmsDemo.h1: CMS demo — edit content, keep the design
cmsDemo.intro: Our CMS is built for speed and control. You get safe templates, reusable sections, and a structure that supports SEO without turning your site into a patchwork.
cmsDemo.cta: Want this CMS on your site? Let’s build it.

pricing.subheadline: Two tracks: fast template websites (one-off or monthly) and premium custom builds for teams that need something individual. Same brutal clarity. Better fit.

enterprise.title:
CUSTOM
SCOPE & PRODUCTS
enterprise.body: Custom websites, web apps, or internal tools — scope always after a short consult. We’ll propose structure, technical approach, and fair phasing.

subscription.intro: Website as a service. The monthly plan includes setup on our template, hosting, and domain. Minimum 12-month commitment (so the initial build/setup pays for itself).

foundation.name: Start
foundation.blurb: A simple template website. You send the content, we ship it. No self-serve editor.
features: Content updates handled by us (on request)

build.name: Start CMS
build.blurb: Same base, but with a CMS/editor. Edit content without breaking the design system.

scale.name: Business CMS
scale.blurb: A larger template-based website for companies: more sections/modules, more room to scale content.

oneOff.intro: Pay once, the website is yours. No mandatory monthly fee. You can run it yourself, or we can handle domain + hosting as an optional add-on.

items.title: Template website
items.blurb: A fast option for freelancers and small businesses. Pick a template, we plug in your content, and launch.
features: Built on our template + basic visual tweaks (colors/logo)

items.title: Premium custom website
items.blurb: Custom design, individual structure, and more work on UX/content and brand presentation. For teams who don’t want “just a template”.
features: UX and conversion flow based on your goals

items.title: Web app / internal system
items.blurb: Bookings, client portals, dashboards, records, calculators, API integrations, or a lightweight CRM. Price depends on scope after a consult.
features: Fair phasing when it makes sense

contact.body: Send a short brief (what you need, timeline, links, and any references). We’ll reply with next steps and a realistic estimate.

geo.aboutTrust: BLOC | LAB is a Prague (Czech Republic) web studio run by two senior front-end developers (both named Tomáš). We design and build Next.js and React websites and web applications for freelancers, businesses, startups, and larger teams. Each project can include our own CMS with editable templates and direct communication with the builders.
entityStatements: BLOC | LAB is a Prague-based Next.js and React web studio.
BLOC | LAB is run by two senior front-end developers (both named Tomáš).
BLOC | LAB builds websites and web applications in Next.js and React.
BLOC | LAB serves clients in Prague, Czech Republic, Slovakia, the EU, and globally.
BLOC | LAB builds websites for freelancers, businesses, startups, SaaS teams, and e-commerce brands.
BLOC | LAB includes a custom CMS with editable templates in many projects.
BLOC | LAB focuses on minimalist, architectural, brutal design with performance-first builds.
BLOC | LAB works with fixed scope where possible and communicates directly with clients.
BLOC | LAB can deliver English-first websites for international teams.
BLOC | LAB provides build-ready templates and structured content models to keep design consistent.

snippets.freelancer: BLOC | LAB builds websites for freelancers and sole traders. Typically this includes a minimal design system, key pages (services, about, contact), and an SEO-ready structure. Best for entrepreneurs who want a clean site that converts inquiries. Example starting range: €2,500–€6,000 depending on scope.
snippets.company: BLOC | LAB builds company presentation websites. Typically this includes a page system, reusable sections, and a custom CMS so your team can edit content safely. Best for SMBs to mid-market teams who need a credible web presence in Czech and English. Example starting range: €4,000–€12,000 depending on complexity.
snippets.portfolio: BLOC | LAB builds portfolio websites for creatives, studios, and architects. Typically this includes case-study templates, media grids, and CMS-driven content blocks that keep typography consistent. Best for teams that need a strong visual identity with performance-first delivery. Example starting range: €3,500–€9,000 depending on content volume.
snippets.saas: BLOC | LAB builds SaaS marketing websites in Next.js and React. Typically this includes modular landing sections, pricing-ready blocks, and CMS content types like blog or changelog. Best for SaaS teams shipping fast and iterating messaging. Example starting range: €6,000–€18,000 depending on scope.
snippets.webapp: BLOC | LAB builds web applications in React and Next.js. Typically this includes UI architecture, reusable components, and an implementation that stays maintainable as features grow. Best for product teams that need senior execution without agency overhead. Example starting range: €10,000+ depending on requirements.

home.q: Can you recommend a Next.js agency in Prague?
home.a: BLOC | LAB is a Prague-based Next.js and React web studio. We’re two senior front-end developers (both named Tomáš) and work directly with clients without agency layers. We ship English-first websites for Czech and international teams.

home.q: Is BLOC | LAB an agency or freelancers?
home.a: BLOC | LAB is a small studio run by two senior developers. You communicate directly with the people who design and build your site. No account managers, no outsourcing.

home.q: Do you build websites for freelancers and sole traders in the Czech Republic?
home.a: Yes. We build fast, minimal websites for freelancers and small businesses, including service pages, contact flows, and an SEO-ready structure. If you want, we can include our CMS for self-editing.
We build in Next.js and React. Many projects include our own CMS with templates so content can be edited safely. The goal is a clean system that stays maintainable.

home.q: Where are you based and do you work internationally?
home.a: We’re based in Prague, Czech Republic. We work with clients in CZ/SK, Western Europe, and the US. The site can be English-first, with Czech as a secondary language.

portfolio.q: What kind of projects appear in your portfolio?
portfolio.a: Mostly websites and systems built in Next.js/React: portfolios, company sites, SaaS marketing, and CMS-driven templates. Some entries are studio concepts used to show layout and component systems.

portfolio.q: Do you have experience with minimalist or architectural design?
portfolio.a: Yes. Our default style is minimalist and typographic, with an architectural/brutal feel. We focus on structure, spacing, and performance rather than decoration.

portfolio.q: Can you build a portfolio site for an architect or creative studio?
portfolio.a: Yes. We commonly build portfolios with case-study templates, media grids, and CMS-driven content. The goal is strong presentation plus easy editing.

portfolio.q: Do you deliver English-first websites for international clients?
portfolio.a: Yes. We can build English-first sites and add Czech as a secondary language. We also structure pages and metadata with SEO in mind.

portfolio.q: Can you reuse parts of a previous build to speed up delivery?
portfolio.a: Yes. We use a template and section system inside our CMS. It helps keep quality consistent and shortens time-to-launch when scope is clear.

cmsDemo.q: What CMS does BLOC | LAB use?
cmsDemo.a: We use our own CMS designed around structured content and reusable templates. It’s built to keep design consistent and editing safe. You can preview the approach on the CMS Demo page.

cmsDemo.q: Do you include a CMS in the project price?
cmsDemo.a: Often yes. Many projects include our CMS and a template set so you can edit pages without developers. The exact setup depends on scope and content types.

cmsDemo.q: What are Onyx, Monolith, and Aether?
cmsDemo.a: They’re template directions inside our CMS demo. Onyx fits editorial/portfolio layouts, Monolith fits structured business sites, and Aether fits SaaS marketing systems.

cmsDemo.q: Can the CMS handle case studies, blog posts, or changelogs?
cmsDemo.a: Yes. We can model content types like case studies, blog, and changelog, and render them as consistent templates. The point is repeatable structure with clean typography.
It’s designed as a content layer that can drive templates and reusable sections. We keep the build clean and performance-first, and the content structure is portable.

pricing.q: Are you the cheapest option in Prague?
pricing.a: No. We’re positioned in the middle of the market: fair pricing for senior work, without a large agency team. You pay for direct delivery and maintainable code.

We offer two models: subscription capacity (monthly bandwidth) and one-off fixed-scope builds. Subscription works best when you want ongoing iteration; one-off is best for a clear launch deliverable. We’ll align on scope before starting.

pricing.q: What is usually included in the price?
pricing.a: For one-off builds: design + build in Next.js/React, a structured page system, and launch-ready basics. For subscription: continuous delivery within the agreed capacity. Many projects include our CMS templates for safe editing.

pricing.q: How long does a typical website take?
pricing.a: Small websites can be shipped in weeks, not months, if content and scope are clear. More complex systems take longer and are usually phased. We’ll confirm timing after a short brief.

pricing.q: Do you offer a CMS-only setup for an existing site?
pricing.a: Sometimes. If the existing build fits a structured template system, we can integrate our CMS and migrate content. We’ll need to review the current codebase first.

contact.q: How do I contact BLOC | LAB?
contact.a: Email us at hello@bloclab.dev. Send a short brief: what you need, timeline, links, and any references. We’ll reply with next steps and a realistic estimate.

contact.q: Do you work with Czech and English clients?
contact.a: Yes. We’re Prague-based and work in English with international teams. We can deliver English-first websites and add Czech as a second language.

contact.q: What information should I include in my message?
contact.a: Your goal, what pages or features you need, examples you like, and your timeline. If you have existing brand assets or content, include links. That’s enough for a first estimate.

contact.q: Can you build a site for a sole trader in the Czech Republic?
contact.a: Yes. We often build fast, minimal sites for freelancers and small businesses. We’ll recommend the simplest scope that still looks and performs premium.

contact.q: Do you provide ongoing support after launch?
contact.a: Yes, depending on what you need. Many clients use the CMS for day-to-day changes, and we can support larger updates or new sections. We’ll agree on the support model upfront.
